title: Extend VerilatedVar to hold force control signal pointers
body:
This commit is the updated version of #6705. It makes changes to the Verilated `__Syms` file in order to convey additional information about the relation of a forceable signal to its force control signals without needing to rely on a runtime lookup in the scope's `m_varsp` within `verilated_vpi.cpp`. In my tests, it improved the performance of `vpi_put_value` and `vpi_get_value` on forceable signals by a factor of 6x over #6704, at the cost of slightly more memory usage.

This is done by extending `VerilatedVar` with an owning unique_ptr to a `VerilatedForceControlSignals` struct object, which contains pointers to the `VerilatedVar`s for the `__VforceEn` and `__VforceVal` signals and receives these pointers at compile time through an adapted `varInsert` function. The `unique_ptr` necessitates re-introducing `VerilatedVar`'s move constructor to allow it to be moved into the `m_varsp` map. This commit operates under the assumption that the `VerilatedVarNameMap` is immutable and no signal is ever removed from it. If this assumption is violated, this implementation is not memory safe.

Unlike the original #6705, the `__VforceRd` signal is _not_ part of the `VerilatedForceControlSignals`, since as discussed in #7092, the `__VforceRd` signal may need to be eliminated entirely soon.

No functional change expected.
